mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating unemployment signals deteriorating aggregate demand and impending corporate earnings contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with expanding housing construction indicate robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet strength, supporting earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS and HOUST signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained inflation environment, rising industrial production indicates firms are successfully expanding capacity without triggering demand-pull inflation, boosting corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises consecutively for two months but SPX 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation exceeding the Federal Reserve's target threshold forces prolonged restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of calendar months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate signals underlying economic momentum that translates directly into earnings acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth remains above trend for two consecutive quarters but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth alongside a stable unemployment rate confirms labor market tightness without recession risk, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of walk-forward test instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a financial stress regime, declining consumer credit balances signal reduced household liquidity and impending consumption weakness, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines for three consecutive months but SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations.
